item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a test to determine if any one of these goats were instances of SCP-4665-1, one had its anomalous property successfully activated, and the rest were designated as SCP-4665-1-1 through SCP-4665-1-6.

I feel like this line is unnecessary, especially because it's pretty clunky.

Their plight had lead to the warping of their Buddhist religion into something of a Sarkic-based mockery that derided Hinduism.

You mean "led" instead of "lead" here. Anyways, this is a good article! Not what I was expecting when I saw that there was a new skip on the Wilson's Wildlife Solutions Hub, but a pleasant surprise nonetheless. In a roundabout way, this is an interaction between WWS and GRU-P, which is great for me own little pet project to have WWS interact with all other GoIs in some fashion. But beyond selfish reasons, this also is just a fun little mystery to follow, which is pretty humorous and interesting. "Goats that die if you stare at them" is a pretty amazing image to begin with and you expand upon it well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To answer your question: If you ingest the flowers, you can be killed with a stare. It does not turn you into a goat. If it did, it would have been mentioned in the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm. So if you ingest the flowers, you turn into a goat that can be killed with a stare? And the guy "bumped off" his teammates for money. It's well made, but I don't think I can up vote it here. It kinda feels like its lacking something.

item.title: Take care of the goats
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed reading this, even in its earliest drafts. What really stood out was the darker twist towards the end. The interview may seem more expository than dialogue should be, but it's very effective in delivering the story of this anomaly in a fast and efficient manner.

And I mean… goats, man. They're the greatest of all time.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wasn't initially planning on releasing this SCP on the 4th, but here we are.

  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chamaemelum_nobile_002.JPG

SPECIAL THANKS:
anamazingfantasy, RockTeethMothEyes, AKFrost, and Nameless Mediocre for reviewing
